description: Email draft for David used heavy formatting (bold headers, dash-separated structure) and didn't match David's actual writing voice. Overstructured and read like a Jarvis briefing, not a David email. Also swung from em-dashes to excessive regular dashes, which wasn't the fix David wanted.
correction: David's emails are conversational prose, not formatted briefs. Match his natural cadence. The em-dash rule means use hyphens where grammatically needed, not to restructure every sentence around dashes.
systemic_fix: When drafting emails as David: write natural conversational prose. No bold headers, no bullet-heavy structure, no dash-heavy sentence construction. Read like a human exec wrote it, not a system generated it. Review sent items for David's actual patterns before drafting.
